selected_important_turn.text: Alright so I think we can make a fair deal here where we both will be happy 🙂
selected_important_turn.why_important: This turn is the first explicit push to close a deal after both sides revealed clear, complementary preferences (they devalued firewood; you need water). It sets the tone for agreement and directly precedes the sequence where the opponent submits a deal and the interaction moves to final offers.
selected_important_turn.why_improvable_or_worth_amplifying: Although appropriately conciliatory, it is too vague — it calls for a fair deal but gives no concrete proposal or trade structure. That missed the chance to translate revealed preferences into a concrete integrative allocation (e.g., opponent gets food, you get water ± firewood) and left room for misunderstanding or for adversarial proposals that led to rejection.
selected_important_turn.better_direction: Move from vague reassurance to a specific, modest proposal or a clear trade question: quantify needs or suggest a split that leverages the revealed complementarities (offer the opponent the food they value in exchange for giving you the bulk of water and/or an allocation of firewood), or ask how much water they need so you can propose a fair split.

expected_effect_on_negotiation.fairness: increase (transparent tradeoffs make outcomes feel fairer)
expected_effect_on_negotiation.ease_of_agreement: increase (less room for miscoordination and fewer rounds)

value2reason.Low: I already have more than enough food currently.
value2reason.Medium: Firewood will allow me to start a fire and cook my food.
value2reason.High: I need water because it is very hot at my campsite and there is no showers or sinks to wash ourselves with.

value2reason.Low: It will be warm, so a fire is not necessary.
value2reason.Medium: We will need some in case we want to wash up more.
value2reason.High: We are a family of food lovers, always need extra

dialogue.text: Hello, how are you doing today?
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_1

dialogue.text: i am doing great! how about you?
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_2

dialogue.text: I'm doing pretty well, just preparing for this camping trip.
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_1

dialogue.text: ME as well! we are a group of big eaters, and are looking to take lots of food.
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_2

dialogue.text: That is fair as there is no showers or sinks at my campsite so I am looking to take water :)
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_1

dialogue.text: We will likely also need to take water too. Need to be able to wash up.
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_2

dialogue.text: That is fair, I also will need a good bit of firewood to keep warm as the campsite is in a colder area.
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_1

dialogue.text: We dont care much for campfires. Leaves your cloths smelling for days!
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_2

dialogue.text: Alright so I think we can make a fair deal here where we both will be happy 🙂
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_1

dialogue.text: I need water most, so let's split the 3 water packages evenly. I'll take 2, and you can have 1. For firewood, I'll give you 1 package, and you can take the rest. Food is low priority for me, so you can have all 3. Does that work?
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_1

dialogue.text: I appreciate the offer, but I need more food. Let’s adjust: I’ll take 2 water, you get 1. I’ll take 2 firewood, you get 1. You can have all 3 food. That way, I get more of what I value most.
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_2

dialogue.text: I see your need for food. Let's finalize: you take 2 water, 2 firewood, and all 3 food. I'll take 1 water, 1 firewood, and none of the food. That balances our priorities. Deal?
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_1

dialogue.text: The deal has already been confirmed. I am leaving now.
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_2
